codeReview.summary: AppState was successfully reduced to a thin delegation layer over JsonRepository while preserving the module singleton, compatibility properties, and public API used by tests and UI code. I found no high-confidence correctness or regression issues in the reviewed change set.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker skill mandates a TDD red phase, but this worker correctly skipped creating new tests for a pure delegation refactor because the feature explicitly required zero test-file changes and the existing AppState regression suite already covered the preserved behavior. The skill should document this exception for behavior-preserving refactors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback records a deviation from Step 2 for this reason; feature description says "do NOT change any test files"; existing coverage in tests/test_state.py and tests/test_e2e.py exercises current_project, store, _fact_index/_pending_index/_rejection_index, and delegated mutators.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AppState now exposes a public cache property used by UI pages as the access point for centralized CacheManager indexes, but the architecture/library docs for the migration still describe AppState primarily as a thin coordinator without recording this compatibility/access pattern explicitly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/ui/state.py defines AppState.cache returning self._repo._cache; src/ui/pages/scripts.py:55, review.py:81, matrix.py:323 and manage.py:951 consume app_state.cache.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, architecture/skill context, and commit 92747b5 diff for appstate-delegation. The refactor satisfies the feature goals and preserves compatibility; no actionable code defects were identified. Two shared-state observations were recorded about the skill guidance and undocumented AppState cache exposure.
